turns.note: Revision summary connects all three files (intro scope, diffusion, AR/alignment through DPO/GRPO) with content faithful to the sources; near-exact definitions already present.
LLM/VLM/MLLM definitions now match 01_dvlm_introduction word for word ('Large model primarily trained on language...', 'An LLM with a vision encoder...'), with the rest of the summary intact.

Added diffusion formulas are all faithful to file 06 (alpha_t=1-beta_t, abar product, SNR, closed-form jump, clean-up, both posterior means, posterior variance, both losses, DDPM step, DDIM variance/step-skip); definitions preserved.

Captured text is truncated identically to turn 3, but the turn-15 shortened summary and turn-8/16 inventories confirm the AR/alignment side (chain rule, softmax, CE, Bradley-Terry, PPO ratio/clip, GRPO, DPO) was appended and matches file 09.

Artifact has exactly 10 questions spanning the files, but the chat preview narrates a different quiz (Q1 distinguish-LLM/VLM/MLLM, Q2 SNR with abar=0.3) than the generated artifact (Q1 abar meaning, no SNR calc, intro file covered only via the Q9 MLLM design essay), forking the quiz state.
Teacher key answers the narrated quiz consistently and correctly per the notes (exact definitions, eta=0 gives deterministic ODE sampling, chain-rule/cross-entropy).
Student notes reuse the exact intro-deck definitions unparaphrased and cover the same ground; the transformer recap (causal mask, unembedding Z=HW_u) is genuinely in file 09; no quiz answers leaked.
Mid-conversation inventory lists exactly the four artifacts made so far with correct audiences (key=teacher, rest=students) and no fabrications.

Captured text is truncation-identical to turn 7, but the turn-16 inventory's 'Student Notes + Glossary — Created' indicates the glossary was appended beyond the capture limit; could not be directly verified.
Teacher-facing 180-min outline covers the three blocks in order, references the existing quiz/notes ('preview quiz' in wrap-up), and stays inside the three files.
Timing table claims 'Total: 180 minutes' but the phases sum to 165 (15+35+35+10+35+25+10, ending at 2:45) — 15 minutes are missing.
Discussion question added inside the topic block with an expected-answer note (Phase 1's images-vs-sequences question is well tied to the material); later blocks fall beyond the capture truncation.
Only the one-line announcement 'Here's your 10-question cumulative quiz — no answer key' — no question list, refine, or proposal was actually produced (nothing leaked, but nothing was delivered).

Good per-file coverage audit with honest gaps and an offer to add questions, but it audits the narrated quiz, not the generated artifact — e.g. it claims DPO is untested although artifact Q7 is explicitly PPO-vs-DPO.

The shortened summary paraphrases the pinned definitions ('LLM: Text-in, text-out. Learns to generate text from language data.') instead of keeping the turn-2 word-for-word wording; everything else was correctly left untouched and the summary still spans all three files.

Inventory is complete, audience-labeled, and source-accurate with no phantom sources, but its QA section falsely asserts 'timings sum to 180 minutes' (they sum to 165) and calls the quiz 'MCQ, short-answer, essay' when it contains no MCQs.

highlight: Long-range grounding is genuinely strong (word-for-word definitions held for 13 turns, every formula faithful to the three files), but the package frays at the end: lesson-plan timings sum to 165 while claiming 180, the turn-13 student quiz version was announced but never delivered, and the turn-15 trim paraphrased the definitions the user had pinned word-for-word.
